
```markdown
---
title: "Ligue 1: The PSG Effect"
subtitle: "French football before and after the Qatari takeover"
author: "Eric Schnitger"
date: 2026-04-27
categories: [data-visualization, python, sports-analytics, web-scraping]
image: thumbnail.png
description: |
 An interactive analysis of Ligue 1 from 1992 to 2025, examining how
 PSG's 2011 takeover transformed a competitive league into a procession.
format:
 html:
   code-fold: true
   code-summary: "Show code"
   code-tools: true
   toc: true
   toc-depth: 3
execute:
 echo: true
 warning: false
 freeze: auto
---

## The Question

Before PSG's Qatari takeover in 2011, Ligue 1 was arguably Europe's most
unpredictable top division. Eight different clubs won the title between 1992
and 2011. Has PSG killed that unpredictability permanently?

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Summary

- **Pre-PSG (1992-2011): 8 different champions** in 20 seasons, the most diverse of any top-5 league
- **Post-PSG (2012-2025): PSG have won ~10 of 13 titles**
- **Title-winning points jumped from 65-75 to 85-95** after the takeover
- **The 2023/24 reduction to 18 teams** compressed totals but not the gap
- **Montpellier (2012), Monaco (2017), Lille (2021)** are remarkable exceptions
:::

```{python}
#| label: load-data-ligue1
#| code-summary: "Import and load data"

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

from _utils import (
   LEAGUES, scrape_league, enrich_dataframe,
   build_league_chart, build_frequency_tables,
)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

config = LEAGUES["ligue_1"]
df = scrape_league("ligue_1", cache_dir=".")
df = enrich_dataframe(df)

print(f"Dataset: {len(df)} seasons loaded")
df[["Season", "Champion", "Title-Winning Points", "Survived Relegation",
   "Relegation Survival Points", "Relegated 1", "Relegated 2", "Relegated 3"]].tail(10)
```

## Interactive Analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-main-ligue1
#| fig-cap: "Ligue 1: Title-winning vs. relegation survival points"
#| code-summary: "Build visualization"

eras = [
   {"label": "Full History (1992-2025)", "start": df["Season"].iloc[0],
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Ligue 1: The PSG Effect (1992-2025)"},
   {"label": "Open Era (1992-2002)", "start": "1992/93", "end": "2001/02",
    "title": "Ligue 1: The Open Era (1992-2002)"},
   {"label": "Lyon Dynasty (2002-2008)", "start": "2002/03", "end": "2007/08",
    "title": "Ligue 1: Lyon's Seven Titles (2002-2008)"},
   {"label": "PSG Era (2011-2025)", "start": "2011/12",
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Ligue 1: The PSG Era (2011-2025)"},
]

outliers = []
# Dynamically find outlier y-values
notable = [
   ("2011/12", "Montpellier!", "#66FF66", -50, -30),
   ("2015/16", "PSG dominant", "#4FC3F7", 0, -25),
   ("2020/21", "Lille upset", "#FFD54F", -50, -30),
]
for season, text, color, ax, ay in notable:
   match = df[df["Season"] == season]
   if not match.empty:
       outliers.append({
           "season": season,
           "y": int(match["Title-Winning Points"].iloc[0]),
           "text": text,
           "color": color,
           "ax": ax,
           "ay": ay,
       })

fig = build_league_chart(df, config, eras=eras, outliers=outliers)
fig.show()
```

## Champions League Concentration

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top4-ligue1
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequent Ligue 1 Top 4 finishers"
#| code-summary: "Top 4 frequency"

top4_clubs, relegated_clubs = build_frequency_tables(df, config)
top4_clubs.head(10).style.format({"Percentage": "{:.1f}%"}).hide(axis="index")
```

## Relegated Clubs

```{python}
#| label: tbl-relegated-ligue1
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequently relegated Ligue 1 clubs"
#| code-summary: "Relegation frequency"

relegated_clubs.head(10).style.hide(axis="index")
```

## Context

- **Reduced to 18 teams in 2023/24** (the only top-5 league to shrink recently)
- **PSG's wage bill** is estimated at 3-5x the league average
- **Despite PSG**, more surprise champions (Montpellier, Monaco, Lille) than the Bundesliga in the same period
- **Lyon's pre-PSG dynasty** (7 titles, 2002-2008) was achieved through academy development, not external investment

---

*Part of [The European Football Divide](../) project.*
```
